actionable_feedback: Regenerate ore_reconcile_2.xlsx as a standard .xlsx that spreadsheet tools and openpyxl can open; ensure numeric results are produced by cell formulas (not only hard-coded values) so reviewers can inspect formulas programmatically.

Add explicit per-row columns in the spreadsheet: absolute_variance_flag (ABS(plant_grade_pct - model_grade_pct) > 12) and geology_review_flag (true/false), and populate them for baseline and for each remediation scenario (O1/O2/O3) so reviewers can see which records cross the 12 pp threshold under each option.

In expert_memo.md, include a short verification snippet showing one representative spreadsheet row with the exact cell references and formulas used (e.g., =ABS(B3-C3) for variance, =D3*(C3-B3) for metal impact), and show the computed values from those cells to demonstrate linkage between memo and workbook.

Document in the memo a canonical final score for the attempt (e.g., 'final_score = 0.75') and note the deterministic artifact-contract precheck score separately to avoid confusion. If any numeric 'score' top-level field is included in future outputs, ensure it matches the canonical final score or is explicitly labeled.

If a manual OOXML writer was used, add a brief note in the memo describing how formulas are encoded and attach a small screenshot or textual dump of the cell formulas for at least 2 rows and one scenario to support reproducibility without requiring openpyxl.
Double-check and, if possible, re-run the deterministic precheck after producing the new workbook to confirm programmatic parsing (openpyxl) succeeds in the reviewer environment.
artifact_specific_comments: ore_reconcile_2.xlsx: Ensure formulas are written into cells (not only rendered values). Add explicit flag columns and ensure scenario variations are implemented by cell formulas referencing the packet inputs or a scenario-adjusted input sheet.

expert_memo.md: Add a short verification snippet that points to specific workbook cells and shows the formula text and resulting value for at least one baseline row and one remediation scenario. State the canonical final score clearly.
feedback_summary: Artifacts are present and calculations in the memo are correct, but reproducibility and traceability are weak: the spreadsheet's in-cell formulas could not be programmatically verified (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo/spreadsheet do not show explicit per-row geology_review/absolute_variance_flag columns for baseline and for each remediation scenario. Also, grader outputs contain inconsistent score fields (deterministic artifact score 1.0 vs model final_score 0.75); clarify canonical score in the revision.

recommended_acceptance_checks_for_revisi: openpyxl can parse ore_reconcile_2.xlsx and returns no parse errors
spreadsheet contains formula-bearing cells for variance and metal impact (visible via cell.formula)
spreadsheet includes absolute_variance_flag and geology_review_flag populated for baseline and O1/O2/O3
expert_memo.md contains a verification snippet (cell refs + formulas) and a canonical 'final_score' statement
extra_model_fields.verifier_note: Reconcile 'score' reporting: top-level 'score' value conflicts with 'final_score' and 'model_score' in grader outputs; worker should document canonical final score in the memo.
